title: Load Definitions Facility for DB2 on iSeries
breadcrumbs: Load Definitions Facility for DB2 on iSeries
text: Load Definitions Facility for DB2 on iSeries The Load Definitions functionality is supported for DB2 on iSeries. Functionality The Load Definitions facility for DB2 loads tables, views, columns, and indexes. It does not load relationships. Configuration To load definitions of physical files residing in a normal library: Specify the QSYS2 library using the $DB2I path in the [PATHS] section of the Uniface assignment file: [PATHS] $DB2I TCP:iSeries+Port|OS-user|OS-password + DB2:Rdbdire:QSYS2|| Copy For definitions of physical files that reside in a normal library—rather than a collection—you cannot specify the name of this library in the assignment file path, because a normal library does not contain a SYSTABLES table that can be queried. Instead, specify the QSYS2 library, which has a SYSTABLES catalog table that contains references to all files on the system. The Load Definitions facility will display all files owned by the specified user in all libraries on the system. Note: When loading a physical file that does not have a key, or has a key that does not need to be unique, the entity is created without a key. You must assign key fields manually after the entity has been created. To load definitions residing in a collection: For tables that reside in collections created using the create collection or create schema SQL command, direct the DB2I path in the Uniface assignment file to point to their collection. For QSYS2 collections, specify the $DB2I path in the [PATHS] section of the IDE assignment file, or Uniface Server (UServer) assignment file: To specify the $DB2I path in the IDE client assignment file: [PATHS] $DB2I TCP:iSeries+Port|OS-user|OS-password + DB2:Rdbdire:QSYS2|| Copy To specify the $DB2I path in the [PATHS] section of the Uniface Server assignment file: [PATHS] $DB2I DB2:Rdbdire:QSYS2|| Copy When selecting your database in the Tables tab in the Uniface IDE, use the connector with the name DB2I: (DB2 iSeries) , which will use the DB2I path. For more detailed instructions, see Import Data Definitions From a DBMS . Note: To see the tables from a user other than the OS-user, use Server mode. For more information, see Configuring DB2 Database Access on iSeries . Data Type Mapping The Load Definitions utility maps DB2 data types to Uniface data types and packing codes. The following table lists these mappings. Table 1. Load Definitions Support for DB2 Data Types DB2 Data Type Supported by Load Definitions Uniface Data Type Uniface Packing Code Remarks bigint Yes N I8 Range -9,223,372,036,854,775,808 to 9,223,372,036,854,775,807 integer Yes N I4 Range -2,147,483,648 to 2,147,483,647 smallint Yes N I2 Range -32,768 to 32,767 char, character Yes S C n n <=254 varchar, long varchar Yes S VC n n >=255 clob Yes S SC* blob Yes R SR* graphic Yes S W n vargraph, varg, vargraphic and long vargraphic Yes S VW n dlbclob Yes S SW* date Yes D D Uniface does not allow BC dates. time Yes T T Uniface accuracy is limited to ticks. timestamp, timestmp Yes E E Uniface accuracy is limited to ticks. decimal( p , s ) and numeric( p , s ) Yes N N( p +1). s , N( p +1) p is precision (total number of digits); for Uniface p must be 30 max, field is skipped with a warning if p is larger. s is scale (number of decimal places); for Uniface s must be smaller than p , field is skipped with a warning if they are equal. s =0 generates N( p +1), rather than N( p +1).0 A ValRep is generated to enforce the exact DBMS max/min values during data entry and store validation; the ValRep range depends on p and s . float, real, decfloat Yes F F4 (Length <=4) or F8 (Length>4) Uniface allows -1e+38 to 1e+38 Uniface will read the full database range. Storing outside the Uniface range generates an error. double Yes F F8 Uniface allows -1e+38 to 1e+38 Uniface will read the full database range. Storing outside the Uniface range generates an error. Other data types The following data types are skipped with a warning message TYPE_NOT_SUPPORTED: BINARY VARBIN ARRAY VARBINARY CURSOR DFS_EXTERNAL_INPUT_LIBRARY_TYPE NLS_STRING_UNITS_TYPE REFERENCE ROW XML Fields with a data type that is not known in the service are skipped with a warning message: TYPE_NOT_RECOGNIZED. Related Links Import Data Definitions From a DBMS For some databases, you can import the data dictionary definitions of an existing database into the application model using the Load Definitions facility, which is provided in Project Editor, in the Tables tab of the Resource Browser. Load Definitions Facility The IDE provides a facility for loading database table definitions from the data dictionary of the selected DBMS into the Uniface model. This is useful when you are creating a Uniface application that is designed to access an existing database. Known as the Load Definitions facility, it is located in the Tables tab of the Project Editor's Resource Browser. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
